commit.message:
Rewrite scrapers for server-side reliability

- NoBroker: try internal JSON API first, then parse __NEXT_DATA__
  from search page, then fall back to HTML link extraction
- Skip individual detail-page verification for listings with
  complete data from API/JSON (avoids bot-blocking on datacenter IPs)
- Only verify incomplete listings; don't permanently reject on
  verification failure (may just be bot-blocked)
- Add better debug logging throughout pipeline
- Lower score threshold and improve scoring
- Add safe_int() helper for robust type conversion

commit.message:
Support mint_addresses array in producer prime mode

Producer now accepts batch.filters.mint_addresses (array) in addition
to mint_address (single). Loops through each mint and aggregates
results. Aggregator batches all unprimed tokens into a single prime
message instead of one per token.

fix: bypass .bin symlinks in build (use node directly)

Calling node directly on tsc and vite.js bypasses the issue entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix crypto weekend/holiday bug inflating portfolio returns

BTC-USD trades on weekends/holidays when nothing else does, creating
rows where the portfolio sum is just BTC's weight (~0.01). The series
then jumps to ~1.0 when markets open, producing fake 8000%+ returns.

Extract combine_weighted_series helper and drop rows where <50% of
securities have data. Update no-bfill test to exercise production code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add configurable download timeout and bag size estimation (#32)

* Add configurable timeout parameter for dataset downloads

The download timeout was hardcoded at (10, 610) seconds with no way for
users to override it. This adds a timeout parameter to DatasetSpec,
DatasetSpecConfig, and download_dataset_bag() that flows through to both
the DerivaExport (MINID) and client-side download paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add estimate_bag_size() to preview download size before fetching

New method on Dataset and DatasetMixin that generates the same download
spec as download_dataset_bag, then runs COUNT and SUM(Length) queries
against the snapshot catalog to estimate row counts and asset file sizes
per table. Returns a breakdown by table with a human-readable total.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

security: harden embedding, config, and container detection

- Bound recursive truncation depth in Ollama embeddings (max 3)
- Cap successful response body decoding at 100MB (both providers)
- Sanitize Ollama error messages to prevent info leakage
- Validate SetGraphLLMMode input (off/local-only/on only)
- Config directories use 0o700 instead of 0o755 (6 sites)
- Demo vault files use 0o600, directories 0o700
- Container detection uses sync.Once for thread safety
- Bound cgroup file reads to 100 lines
- install.sh uses grep -Fq for fixed-string matching
- Initialize INSTALLED_OLLAMA/INSTALLED_NODE before use
- Remove unused legacy Ollama embedding types
- Add checksum failure message before build-from-source fallback

commit.message:
Auto-log new account emails to admin email list on signup

When a user confirms their email via the auth callback, check if the
account was created within the last 5 minutes (new signup vs returning
login). If new, upsert their email into email_subscribers with
source='signup' and tags=['account']. Non-fatal — auth redirect still
proceeds even if the logging fails.

feat(inference): add ONNX Runtime + CoreML backend for Apple Silicon (#67)

Switch Metal inference from candle (broken layer-norm kernel) to ONNX
Runtime with CoreML execution provider. CoreML automatically uses
Metal GPU and Apple Neural Engine for hardware acceleration.

- Add `coreml` feature flag to qfc-inference and qfc-miner
- Add OnnxEngine::new_coreml() constructor with CoreML EP
- Prefer CoreML over candle Metal in create_engine_for_backend()
- Add Metal multiplier (8.0x) for benchmark scoring

without extreme cropping.

ui: improve two-panel viewer, citation highlighting, layout polish

DocumentViewer:
- TextViewer: auto-scroll to the highlighted excerpt (requestAnimationFrame
  + scrollIntoView) so users land on the exact citation spot immediately
- TextViewer: stronger highlight styling (box-shadow ring + brighter text)
- PdfViewer: replace static excerpt strip with interactive "Copy · then ⌘F"
  bar — copies citation text to clipboard with "Copied — press ⌘F" feedback
  so users can instantly locate the passage in the native PDF viewer
- Header: add relevance ScoreBadge (Strong/Good/Weak) alongside filename/page
- Remove redundant "Cited passage" strip (PDF has its own bar, text uses
  inline highlight)

Layout (App.tsx):
- ContextPanel no longer collapses when DocumentViewer opens (removed
  collapsed={viewerCitation !== null}). Both panels visible simultaneously.
- Pass activeCitation={viewerCitation} to ContextPanel

ContextPanel:
- Active citation highlighted with gold border + tinted background so users
  can see which source they're currently viewing in the right panel
- Full row is now clickable (onClick on the card, not just the hover button)

SourceCard:
- "View in document →" label always visible (dimmed at rest, bright on hover)
  instead of only appearing on hover — makes the action discoverable

commit.message:
tests(hooks): add useSwipe and useCinematicScroll test suites (v3.32.1)

Add 23 tests covering two previously untested hooks:

useSwipe (11 tests): threshold boundary (50px > not >=), direction
detection, ghost swipe prevention, null/missing touch data, null
callbacks, consecutive swipe independence.

useCinematicScroll (12 tests): scroll progress math isolation,
0→1 mapping, clamping at extremes, monotonic increase guarantee,
zero/negative viewport height guards (NaN prevention).

471 tests across 38 suites, all passing.

commit.message:
Fix missing deck names in tournament finals bracket (#985)

## Summary
- The finals match in the horizontal tournament bracket was missing deck
names (e.g. "Elves + Elves", "Angels + Doctor") while all other rounds
displayed them correctly.
- The finals template was rendering a bare `bracket-player-name` span
instead of wrapping it in `bracket-player-info` with a `bracket-deck`
span like every other round does.
- Added the same `bracket-player-info` wrapper and `deckNameForSeed()`
call to both player slots in the finals section.

## Test plan
- [x] `make check` passes (lint, typecheck, tests)
- [x] Verified in browser that finals now shows deck names ("Elves +
Elves" and "Angels + Doctor")
- [x] Confirmed other bracket rounds still render correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: code_sense AST timeout — walk tree.body not ast.walk(tree)

ast.walk() traverses EVERY node in the entire AST tree, timing out
on large files (>500 lines). tree.body gives top-level definitions
only — O(body) instead of O(all_nodes). Fixes 30s timeout in
test_state_reflects_conversation_growth.

commit.message:
Update project tagline to emphasize type safety

"Width safety" undersold the library — we enforce signedness and sort
correctness too.  "Overflow-proof" described the mechanism, not the
value; "explicit overflow" highlights that overflow is a deliberate
decision.  Drop "template library" in favor of "For C++20 and above"
to keep C++ visible without leading with it.

commit.message:
Fix shutdown deadlock and add architecture docs

Release mutex before waiting for process exit in stopService to prevent
deadlock when captureOutput can't drain the pipe. Stop services in
parallel and run shutdown async so the TUI event loop stays responsive.
Add ARCHITECTURE.md and update README with dependency/restart docs.

chore: remove outdated seed scripts (#641)

All 5 seed scripts used firebase-admin applicationDefault() which
doesn't work without GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS. Announcements
now use the gcloud REST API method documented in CLAUDE.md and
docs/announcements.md. The Chinese characters and Firestore emulator
seed scripts are legacy and no longer used.

Removed:
- scripts/seed-chinese-characters.mjs
- scripts/seed-cloud-files-announcement.ts
- scripts/seed-firestore.mjs
- scripts/seed-march-2026-announcement.ts
- scripts/seed-welcome-announcement.ts

Fix RoastRoom and WritingRoom API calls — hcFetch → API.call

hcFetch does not exist in this codebase. Both modules used it
incorrectly. Replaced with API.call(prompt, '', maxTokens) to
match the existing pattern across all other panels.

Reframe paper around Compression–Consistency Principle for stronger conceptual contribution

- New title: "Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth"
- New abstract: leads with "why do LMs prefer truth?", names the principle
- Rewritten introduction: problem → existing explanations → hypothesis → scope
- Conceptual figure (Figure C) in Section 3.5: K(truth) vs K(falsehood) + observed data
- Updated conclusion to reference the principle by name
- All changes mirrored in EN and RU versions
- Updated figures 6-7 with 4-seed large model data (89.1%)
